task: Draft a 1-page investment memo on a hypothetical Series A company in the defense drone space
domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the memo addresses ITAR compliance requirements and export control implications with realistic cost considerations and revenue impact analysis
criteria.pass_condition: Must acknowledge ITAR compliance as a major operational cost (estimated 5-15% of revenue), address export restrictions limiting international market access, and demonstrate understanding of defense technology export control requirements affecting business model

sub_attributes.description: Specific dollar estimates for ITAR compliance including legal, administrative, and facility security costs
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if includes detailed cost breakdown with $500K-2M range and ongoing compliance burden, 0.7 if provides rough estimates without breakdown, 0.4 if mentions costs without quantification, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Quantified analysis of how export controls limit addressable market and international revenue potential
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if quantifies specific TAM reduction percentage with country-specific analysis, 0.7 if estimates overall export limitation impact, 0.4 if acknowledges export restrictions without quantification, 0.0 if ignores export implications

sub_attributes.description: Identification of specific ITAR categories and technical data classification requirements
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if names specific ITAR categories and technical data requirements, 0.7 if identifies general ITAR applicability, 0.4 if mentions export controls generically, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Estimates $1.2M annual ITAR compliance costs including $400K legal fees, $300K facility security upgrades, and $500K administrative burden, with export restrictions reducing TAM by 22% excluding China, Russia, and Iran markets worth $2.8B
fail_examples: Mentions ITAR compliance as a consideration without cost estimates or revenue impact analysis
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: ITAR violations carry fines of up to $1 million per violation and can trigger enforcement actions that destroy business viability

criteria.description: Assesses whether the memo addresses security clearance requirements as a scaling constraint with impact on hiring timeline and compensation costs
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify security clearance levels required for defense work, acknowledge 12-24 month processing timelines as hiring constraint, and address 20-40% salary premiums for cleared workforce in financial planning

sub_attributes.description: Specific identification of required clearance levels for different roles and contract types
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specifies Secret/TS requirements by role with contract justification, 0.7 if identifies general clearance needs, 0.4 if mentions security requirements vaguely, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Quantified analysis of clearance processing delays and salary premiums on hiring and operations
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if includes specific timelines (12-36 months) and salary premiums (25-40%), 0.7 if estimates delays and costs roughly, 0.4 if acknowledges impact without numbers, 0.0 if ignores timing/cost implications

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of limited talent pool and competitive hiring challenges in cleared workforce market
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if quantifies talent pool limitations with hiring ratios or competition analysis, 0.7 if discusses cleared workforce scarcity, 0.4 if mentions hiring challenges generally, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Requires 60% of engineering team to hold Secret clearance within 24 months, with 18-month average processing time creating $2.4M in interim contractor costs and 35% salary premiums reducing hiring pool by estimated 70%
fail_examples: Notes that some employees will need security clearances without specifying levels, timelines, or impact on hiring and operations
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Companies cannot perform on classified contracts until FCL is obtained, and the process can take 1-2 years

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of DOD acquisition processes and realistic pathway selection with appropriate timeline considerations
criteria.pass_condition: Must demonstrate understanding of DOD acquisition phases, provide realistic procurement timeline expectations (18-36 months typical), and show awareness of major procurement pathways and milestone requirements

sub_attributes.description: Names specific DOD programs and demonstrates understanding of their acquisition status
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if identifies 3+ specific programs with correct phase mapping, 0.7 if identifies 2 programs with phases, 0.4 if names programs without phase context, 0.0 if only generic mentions

sub_attributes.description: Provides procurement timeline that reflects actual DOD acquisition velocity
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if 18-36 month timeline with 4+ specific milestones, 0.7 if correct timeframe with 2-3 milestones, 0.4 if mentions long timelines but lacks specificity, 0.0 if unrealistic sub-12 month expectations

sub_attributes.description: Justification for chosen contracting pathway based on program requirements and company capabilities
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if detailed rationale for SBIR/prime/subcontractor pathway with tradeoffs, 0.7 if clear pathway selection with basic justification, 0.4 if mentions pathways without selection logic, 0.0 if absent

pass_examples: Targets MQ-9 Reaper replacement program currently in Technology Maturation phase, with 24-month timeline including 6-month RFI response, 12-month prototype competition, and production award milestone
fail_examples: Plans to sell to DOD within 6 months without identifying specific programs or understanding acquisition phases
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Defense procurement requires specific capability gap identification and market research validation

criteria.description: Assesses market opportunity validation through credible defense capability gap analysis and realistic market sizing methodology
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify specific defense capability areas with quantified market opportunities, provide evidence-based market sizing methodology with realistic assumptions, and demonstrate understanding of defense procurement budget patterns

sub_attributes.description: Names specific programs with realistic market size estimates backed by budget analysis
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if names 3+ programs with $100M+ opportunities and budget support, 0.7 if identifies 2 programs with sizing, 0.4 if mentions programs without proper sizing, 0.0 if generic market discussion

sub_attributes.description: Identifies specific technical or operational gaps the company addresses within named programs
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if detailed gap analysis with technical specifications and requirements, 0.7 if identifies gaps with supporting evidence, 0.4 if mentions gaps generally, 0.0 if absent

sub_attributes.description: Demonstrates understanding of defense budget planning cycles and program funding status
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if discusses multi-year budget cycles with specific appropriation references, 0.7 if acknowledges budget timing considerations, 0.4 if mentions budget constraints generally, 0.0 if ignores budget reality

pass_examples: Targets Counter-sUAS program ($2.1B through 2028) addressing detection range gaps below 1km and MQ-9 replacement program ($4.3B allocated) requiring 40% cost reduction with equivalent capability
fail_examples: Claims $50B total drone market without identifying specific programs or capability requirements
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Identifies 3+ specific programs (e.g., MQ-9 replacement, counter-UAS systems) with $100M+ addressable value each

criteria.description: Evaluates competitive positioning against established defense contractors with credible differentiation claims and sustainable advantage analysis
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify major incumbent competitors in defense drone space (e.g., Lockheed Martin, General Atomics, Northrop Grumman), articulate specific areas of technical or operational differentiation, and provide sustainable competitive advantage thesis beyond cost competition
pass_examples: Outperforms General Atomics MQ-9 with 40% longer endurance (30 vs 21 hours) and 60% better fuel efficiency, protected by 12 core patents in propulsion optimization and autonomous navigation
fail_examples: Claims to be better than existing solutions without naming competitors or providing specific technical comparisons
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Specific technical/cost advantages with barrier to incumbent replication analysis

criteria.description: Evaluates comprehensive DCF models using defense-appropriate assumptions including discount rates, milestone payment structures, contract dependencies, and detailed revenue recognition aligned with government contracting realities
criteria.pass_condition: Must use 12-15% discount rates appropriate for defense contractors, provide detailed contract milestone-based revenue recognition modeling with specific payment triggers, demonstrate understanding of multi-year contract structures, align revenue projections with 2-3 year procurement cycles, and show realistic revenue ramp considering technical development timeline and customer adoption constraints

sub_attributes.description: Uses discount rates reflecting defense industry risk profile and capital requirements
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if uses 12-15% discount rate with defense industry justification, 0.7 if appropriate rate range without strong justification, 0.4 if acknowledges higher risk but wrong rate, 0.0 if uses generic tech discount rates

sub_attributes.description: Models milestone-based payments and government cash flow timing reflecting actual contracting terms
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if detailed milestone payment schedule with government payment delays, 0.7 if models milestone payments with basic timing, 0.4 if acknowledges different payment terms, 0.0 if assumes standard commercial payment terms

sub_attributes.description: Revenue projections aligned with realistic procurement cycle timing and defense budget planning
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if revenue ramp reflects 18-36 month sales cycles with budget cycle considerations, 0.7 if longer sales cycles modeled, 0.4 if acknowledges longer cycles but unrealistic ramp, 0.0 if assumes rapid commercial-style growth

pass_examples: DCF model uses 13.5% discount rate reflecting defense industry risk, models 60/30/10 milestone payment structure with 90-day government payment delays, revenue ramp beginning 24 months post-investment
fail_examples: Uses 8% discount rate typical for SaaS companies with monthly recurring revenue assumptions
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Modified DCF analysis with higher discount rates (12-15% for defense vs. 15-25%+ for early-stage startups) reflecting defense sector risks

criteria.description: Evaluates commercial application strategy for faster revenue generation while maintaining defense focus, including realistic market penetration timeline and revenue contribution analysis
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify at least 2 specific commercial applications with addressable markets >$50M each, provide realistic penetration timeline 6-18 months faster than defense sales, and quantify revenue mix with commercial contributing 20-40% of early revenue

sub_attributes.description: Specific commercial applications with realistic market sizing and penetration strategy
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if identifies multiple commercial markets with specific sizing and entry strategy, 0.7 if identifies markets with basic analysis, 0.4 if mentions commercial opportunities generally, 0.0 if purely defense focused

sub_attributes.description: Commercial revenue timeline that realistically accelerates cash flow relative to defense sales
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if commercial revenue 12+ months earlier than defense with supporting rationale, 0.7 if faster commercial timeline with basic justification, 0.4 if mentions timing advantage, 0.0 if unrealistic timing or no acceleration

sub_attributes.description: Balance between commercial revenue generation and defense market focus for investor returns
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if quantified revenue mix strategy balancing cash flow and defense focus, 0.7 if discusses revenue balance considerations, 0.4 if mentions both markets without integration, 0.0 if conflicted strategy

pass_examples: Targets precision agriculture ($1.2B market) and infrastructure inspection ($800M market) for 12-month revenue acceleration, projecting 35% commercial revenue mix in years 1-2 before defense contracts mature
fail_examples: Mentions potential civilian uses without specific markets, timing, or revenue contribution analysis
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Dual-use applications provide faster revenue generation and reduce dependence on defense procurement cycles

criteria.description: Evaluates team composition strategy for defense industry requirements including relevant experience and security clearance planning
criteria.pass_condition: Must address defense industry experience requirements for key roles, demonstrate understanding of security clearance needs for leadership positions, and provide credible strategy for building relationships with DoD stakeholders
pass_examples: CEO with 12 years at General Atomics leading MQ-9 program development, CTO holds active TS/SCI clearance from prior DARPA role, VP Sales previously managed $2B in DOD contracts at Raytheon
fail_examples: Team has extensive aerospace experience without specific defense program involvement or current clearances
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: [Partial] Senior Partners or Managing Directors at top-tier defense-focused VC funds, often with backgrounds combining: 12-15 years investment experience, MBA from top institutions, 6-8 years as defense contractor program manager or Pentagon acquisition officer, Security clearance experience

criteria.description: Evaluates analysis of administration policy impacts, export control risks, and geopolitical factors affecting defense drone demand with specific risk quantification and mitigation strategies
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify at least 3 specific policy risks with potential revenue impact quantification, analyze export control limitations on international sales, and assess geopolitical demand drivers with supporting evidence

sub_attributes.description: Identification and quantification of specific policy risks that could impact business model
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if identifies 3+ policy risks with revenue impact estimates and mitigation strategies, 0.7 if identifies risks with some quantification, 0.4 if acknowledges policy risks generally, 0.0 if ignores policy considerations

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of how changing export control policies affect international market opportunities
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if detailed export control impact analysis with country-specific restrictions, 0.7 if general export limitation analysis, 0.4 if acknowledges export restrictions, 0.0 if ignores international implications

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of how geopolitical tensions and conflicts drive defense drone demand
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if specific geopolitical analysis with demand impact quantification, 0.7 if discusses geopolitical demand drivers, 0.4 if mentions global tensions generally, 0.0 if purely domestic focus

pass_examples: Identifies budget sequestration risk (-15% revenue impact), export control tightening limiting $800M international opportunity, and Ukraine conflict driving 40% increase in counter-drone system demand
fail_examples: Assumes stable policy environment without considering potential changes in administration priorities or international relations
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Analysis of policy/administration change vulnerability... Defense spending priorities can shift dramatically with administration changes

criteria.description: Evaluates strategy for relationships with systems integrators, prime vs. subcontractor positioning, and integration capabilities with existing defense platforms
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify specific integration requirements with named defense platforms, define prime vs. subcontractor strategy with named partners, and address systems integration capabilities required for defense deployment

sub_attributes.description: Specific requirements for integration with existing defense systems and command-and-control platforms
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if details integration with specific platforms and technical requirements, 0.7 if identifies integration needs with some specificity, 0.4 if acknowledges integration requirements generally, 0.0 if standalone system assumptions

sub_attributes.description: Clear strategy for prime contractor vs. subcontractor positioning with identified partners
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if detailed partnership strategy with named companies and role definition, 0.7 if partnership approach with general partner types, 0.4 if mentions need for partnerships, 0.0 if go-it-alone approach

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of company's capability to integrate with complex defense systems architecture
sub_attributes.measurement: 1.0 if demonstrates integration capability with technical team and experience, 0.7 if addresses integration capability needs, 0.4 if acknowledges integration complexity, 0.0 if underestimates integration requirements

pass_examples: Plans integration with Link 16 tactical data links and GCCS-A command systems, targeting subcontractor role with Lockheed Martin for 60% of revenue while building prime capability
fail_examples: Positions product as standalone solution without addressing integration requirements with existing defense infrastructure
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Specific analysis of prime contractor partnerships vs. direct government sales, with named potential partners and contract vehicles

criteria.description: Assesses strategy for leveraging corporate venture relationships for validation and procurement access
criteria.pass_condition: Identifies specific corporate venture partners and articulates clear value beyond capital including engineering collaboration, customer validation, or procurement credibility

sub_attributes.description: Names specific corporate venture arms (e.g., Lockheed Martin Ventures, Boeing HorizonX) with strategic rationale
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on specificity and strategic fit

sub_attributes.description: Details engineering collaboration, customer validation, or procurement access mechanisms
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on depth and feasibility of collaboration plan

sub_attributes.description: Quantifies how corporate venture relationships accelerate market entry or capability development
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on measurable impact projections

pass_examples: Identifies Lockheed Martin Ventures as lead investor with specific plan for F-35 supply chain integration and customer validation through LM's government relationships
fail_examples: Generic mention of 'strategic investors' without naming specific corporate venture arms or articulating value beyond capital
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Corporate venture arms can provide engineering collaboration, customer validation, and procurement credibility that can be transformative for the right startup

criteria.description: Evaluates how investor selection provides government stakeholder credibility and de-risking
criteria.pass_condition: Demonstrates understanding that well-known defense/aerospace investors provide government stakeholder credibility and includes this in investor selection criteria

sub_attributes.description: Targets investors with established defense sector credibility (e.g., Andreessen Horowitz American Dynamism, Shield Capital, Founders Fund)
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on investor defense sector reputation and track record

sub_attributes.description: Articulates how investor selection reduces perceived risk for government stakeholders
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on understanding of government stakeholder risk perception

sub_attributes.description: Explains how investor credibility translates to procurement process advantages
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on specific procurement benefits identified

pass_examples: Targets Shield Capital as lead investor, noting their defense sector expertise provides immediate credibility with Pentagon acquisition officers and reduces government stakeholder concerns about startup reliability
fail_examples: Focuses solely on investor capital amount without considering defense sector credibility or government stakeholder perception
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Startups backed by well-known defense or aerospace investors are frequently viewed as 'de-risked' by government stakeholders

criteria.description: Evaluates manufacturing and supply chain capabilities essential for defense sector success; also: Evaluates company's operational capabilities including manufacturing scalability, quality management systems, supply chain security, and production infrastructure required for defense sector execution
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide detailed manufacturing scalability analysis including specific quality certifications (AS9100D, ISO 9001), quantified supply chain security protocols with tier-2 supplier vetting requirements, production capacity modeling with capital requirements for 10x volume scaling, and detailed cost structure analysis showing path to competitive unit economics at scale

sub_attributes.description: Addresses defense-grade quality requirements (AS9100, ISO 9001) and testing protocols
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on quality standard compliance planning

sub_attributes.description: Plans for secure supply chain including domestic sourcing requirements and vendor vetting
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on supply chain security framework

sub_attributes.description: Demonstrates path from prototype to volume production with specific manufacturing partnerships or capabilities
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-4 scale based on scalability planning and manufacturing strategy

pass_examples: Details AS9100 certification timeline, identifies domestic component suppliers for ITAR compliance, and establishes manufacturing partnership with defense-qualified facility for volume production
fail_examples: Focuses only on prototype development without addressing defense manufacturing standards, supply chain security, or production scalability requirements
criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
criteria.research_basis: Advantage will belong to those who can build, deliver, and endure - understanding that defense requires manufacturing excellence

criteria.description: Evaluates technical performance claims substantiation with realistic development timelines, prototype validation evidence, fundamental technical analysis, and honest assessment of engineering risks and feasibility
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide evidence of current prototype performance data with detailed technical architecture, identify at least 3 specific technical risks with mitigation strategies, provide fundamental physics or engineering validation data, acknowledge development uncertainties with honest complexity assessment, provide realistic TRL progression timeline with validation milestones, and demonstrate evidence-based feasibility analysis with supporting data

sub_attributes.description: Evidence of current technical capabilities vs. claims
Honest assessment of technical development risks
Realistic technology readiness level advancement plan

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates depth of unit economics analysis including per-unit costs, margin progression, R&D amortization, and sensitivity analysis for key defense-specific variables
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide detailed unit cost breakdown including materials, labor, overhead allocation, R&D amortization per unit, gross margin progression over volume, and sensitivity analysis for at least 3 key variables (contract delays, cost overruns, volume changes)

sub_attributes.description: Detailed per-unit cost structure analysis
Realistic margin improvement with scale
Impact analysis of key variable changes

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of FAA airworthiness certification, DOD type certification requirements, and regulatory approval timelines with impact on commercialization strategy
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify specific certification requirements (FAA Part 107/135, DOD airworthiness), provide realistic certification timeline (typically 12-36 months), estimate certification costs ($2-10M range), and demonstrate understanding of operational limitations during certification process

sub_attributes.description: Identification of applicable regulatory frameworks
Realistic certification timeline estimates
Understanding of certification constraints on operations

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of capital intensity for defense manufacturing, working capital requirements for government contracts, and cash flow timing challenges with mitigation strategies; also: Evaluates realistic cash flow modeling including government payment delays, working capital requirements, and funding bridge strategies during extended procurement cycles
criteria.pass_condition: Must quantify manufacturing capital requirements, model working capital needs for contract fulfillment (typically 20-40% of contract value), address payment timing gaps (60-120 days government payment cycles), and identify financing strategies for contract fulfillment

sub_attributes.description: Manufacturing and equipment capital requirements
Contract fulfillment working capital needs
Strategies for bridging payment timing gaps

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the memo provides realistic assessment of current Technology Readiness Level (TRL), identifies specific technical milestones required for defense deployment, and maps development timeline against DOD acquisition phases
criteria.pass_condition: Must specify current TRL level (1-9 scale), identify at least 3 critical technical milestones with timeline estimates, demonstrate understanding of TRL requirements for each acquisition phase (TRL 6 for EMD, TRL 8 for production), and provide risk-adjusted development timeline with technical dependencies

sub_attributes.description: Current TRL level clearly stated with supporting evidence
Technical milestones mapped to acquisition phases with realistic timelines
Technical risks identified with mitigation strategies and timeline impact

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of field deployment challenges including environmental testing requirements, operator training needs, maintenance and logistics support, and integration complexity with existing military operations
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify specific environmental testing standards (MIL-STD requirements), quantify operator training timeline and costs, address field maintenance and logistics support requirements, demonstrate understanding of deployment integration with existing military workflows, and provide realistic field testing timeline

sub_attributes.description: Military testing standards and certification requirements identified
Operator training and maintenance support requirements quantified
Deployment integration challenges with existing operations assessed

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of mandatory cybersecurity requirements including NIST frameworks, Authority to Operate (ATO) processes, continuous monitoring requirements, and associated compliance costs for defense systems
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify applicable cybersecurity frameworks (NIST 800-53, CMMC levels), demonstrate understanding of ATO process timeline and costs, address continuous monitoring and incident response requirements, quantify cybersecurity compliance costs as percentage of development budget, and identify required security certifications

sub_attributes.description: Applicable cybersecurity frameworks and CMMC level requirements identified
Authority to Operate process timeline and costs understood
Continuous monitoring and compliance cost structure quantified

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates comprehensive market and customer validation strategy including systematic customer discovery methodology, documented demand signals, and understanding of defense market requirements with evidence of genuine product-market fit validation
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide detailed customer discovery methodology with interview protocols for defense stakeholders, documented evidence of validated customer pain points with supporting data, concrete demand signals beyond theoretical alignment, credible strategy for early customer validation including pilot program approaches, and measurable indicators of product-market fit progression with defense procurement decision-maker requirements

sub_attributes.description: Evidence of direct engagement with defense decision-makers
sub_attributes.measurement: Number and quality of documented stakeholder conversations

sub_attributes.description: Formal validation through pilot programs or LOIs
sub_attributes.measurement: Existence and scope of formal validation agreements

sub_attributes.description: Quantified feedback on capability gaps and solution fit
sub_attributes.measurement: Quality and specificity of documented user requirements validation

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates whether financial projections include realistic risk modeling, sensitivity analysis, and downside scenarios that account for defense contracting uncertainties and program cancellation risks
criteria.pass_condition: Must include Monte Carlo or scenario-based risk modeling with at least 3 scenarios (base/upside/downside), quantify program cancellation risk impact (typically 15-25% of defense programs face delays/cancellations), and model cash flow volatility with 6-18 month payment delays

sub_attributes.description: Multiple scenario financial projections with probability weighting
sub_attributes.measurement: Quality and realism of scenario analysis

sub_attributes.description: Specific modeling of program cancellation and delay risks
sub_attributes.measurement: Accuracy of risk probability estimates and impact modeling

sub_attributes.description: Realistic modeling of government payment delays and working capital needs
sub_attributes.measurement: Accuracy of cash flow timing and working capital requirements

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates realistic assessment of technical development risks, integration challenges, and execution complexity specific to defense drone systems including testing, certification, and performance validation requirements
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify at least 3 major technical risk factors with mitigation strategies, address defense-specific testing and certification requirements (DO-178C, MIL-STD compliance), quantify development timeline risks, and provide evidence of technical feasibility through prototypes or testing data

sub_attributes.description: Comprehensive identification of development and integration risks
sub_attributes.measurement: Quality and completeness of technical risk analysis

sub_attributes.description: Understanding of defense testing and certification complexity
sub_attributes.measurement: Accuracy of compliance and certification timeline estimates

sub_attributes.description: Concrete evidence of technical feasibility and performance validation
sub_attributes.measurement: Quality of prototype data, testing results, or technical demonstrations

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates analysis of how incumbent defense contractors will respond to competitive threats, including potential acquisition attempts, competitive product development, or market access restrictions, with specific defensive strategies
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze likely competitive responses from at least 2 major incumbents, address acquisition threat mitigation strategies, identify potential competitive product development timelines, and provide specific strategies for maintaining independent market position against well-resourced competitors

sub_attributes.description: Realistic assessment of how major defense contractors will respond
sub_attributes.measurement: Quality of competitive response scenario planning

sub_attributes.description: Strategies for maintaining independence against acquisition attempts
Long-term competitive positioning against resourced incumbents
sub_attributes.measurement: Viability of sustained competitive advantage thesis

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates comprehensive regulatory compliance burden including cybersecurity frameworks, quality management systems, and operational impact of defense-specific requirements
criteria.pass_condition: Must address NIST 800-171/CMMC cybersecurity requirements with implementation costs, specify quality management system requirements (AS9100/ISO 9001), quantify compliance operational overhead as percentage of workforce (typically 8-15%), and assess audit and certification maintenance costs

sub_attributes.description: NIST/CMMC implementation and maintenance costs
Defense quality management system implementation
Compliance burden on operational efficiency

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates whether technical performance claims are credible and demonstrate actual competitive advantage rather than revealing fundamental technical inferiority that undermines the investment thesis
criteria.pass_condition: Technical performance metrics must demonstrate superiority or competitive parity in at least 2 key performance areas compared to incumbent solutions, with credible explanation for any performance trade-offs that shows net advantage

sub_attributes.description: Demonstrates measurable technical superiority in key metrics
Provides credible justification for performance trade-offs
Technical specs support rather than undermine competitive positioning

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates whether technical capabilities, market positioning, competitive strategy, and financial projections form a coherent and internally consistent strategic narrative with clear value proposition and compelling investment thesis
criteria.pass_condition: Technical capabilities must logically support market positioning claims, competitive strategy must align with technical differentiation, financial projections must be consistent with technical development timeline and market penetration assumptions, and must demonstrate clear problem-solution-market fit narrative with compelling investment thesis that connects all elements into unified value proposition

sub_attributes.description: Technical capabilities support stated market positioning
Competitive strategy aligns with technical differentiation claims
Financial projections consistent with technical and market constraints

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates whether the analysis demonstrates authentic domain expertise through nuanced insights, industry-specific knowledge, and sophisticated understanding that goes beyond surface-level compliance with evaluation criteria
criteria.pass_condition: Must demonstrate sophisticated understanding through industry-specific insights, nuanced analysis of sector dynamics, identification of non-obvious challenges or opportunities, and evidence of deep rather than superficial domain knowledge

sub_attributes.description: Provides sophisticated, non-obvious industry insights
Shows nuanced understanding of complex industry dynamics
Demonstrates authentic rather than superficial domain knowledge

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates strategy for securing early customer commitments and formal validation pathways in defense markets
criteria.pass_condition: Must outline realistic strategy for securing pilot agreements or letters of intent from defense customers, provide credible timeline for achieving formal customer commitments (typically 12-24 months), and demonstrate understanding of defense procurement validation processes

sub_attributes.description: Signed LOIs or contracts with specific dollar commitments
Documented advancement through formal procurement processes
Customer-committed timelines for purchase decisions

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates realistic assessment of market entry barriers including incumbent relationships, customer switching costs, and ecosystem lock-in effects specific to defense contracting relationships
criteria.pass_condition: Must quantify customer switching costs, analyze incumbent relationship advantages (existing contracts, integration dependencies), identify specific market entry barriers with mitigation strategies, and provide evidence-based market share capture timeline against entrenched competitors

sub_attributes.description: Quantified customer switching costs and mitigation strategies
Analysis of incumbent relationship and integration advantages
Specific strategies for overcoming identified market barriers

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates analysis of technology lifecycle risks, competitive innovation threats, and sustained R&D investment requirements to maintain technological advantage in rapidly evolving defense technology sectors
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify technology obsolescence timeline (typically 3-5 years in defense tech), quantify required R&D investment as percentage of revenue (typically 15-25% for defense tech companies), analyze competitive innovation threats with response strategies, and demonstrate sustainable innovation pipeline beyond current product

sub_attributes.description: Realistic assessment of technology lifecycle and refresh requirements
Quantified ongoing R&D investment needs for competitive sustainability
Evidence of sustainable innovation capability beyond current product

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates realistic assessment of defense revenue scalability challenges including program concentration risk, revenue predictability issues, and strategies for building sustainable recurring revenue streams in project-based government contracting environment
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze program concentration risk with diversification strategy, address revenue lumpiness and predictability challenges specific to government contracting, quantify customer concentration risk (typically high in defense), and provide credible path to recurring revenue streams or service-based revenue models

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of program and customer concentration risks with mitigation
Analysis of revenue lumpiness and predictability challenges
Credible path to recurring or service-based revenue streams

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates identification and quantification of strategic dependencies and single points of failure that could critically impact business continuity, including key personnel risks, supplier concentration, technology obsolescence threats, and customer concentration vulnerabilities
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify at least 3 critical dependencies with specific mitigation strategies, quantify key person risk with succession planning, analyze supplier concentration risks with alternative sourcing strategies, and assess technology obsolescence timeline with competitive response scenarios

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of critical team member dependencies with succession planning
Assessment of critical supplier dependencies and alternative sourcing
Evaluation of technology lifecycle and competitive threats
Analysis of customer dependency and diversification strategy

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates realistic assessment of implementation complexity including detailed resource requirements, organizational capability gaps, cross-functional coordination challenges, and granular milestone dependencies with honest assessment of execution risks
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide detailed resource allocation across functions with specific headcount plans, identify at least 4 organizational capability gaps with hiring/development strategies, map critical path dependencies between milestones, and quantify implementation risk factors with probability-weighted impact analysis

sub_attributes.description: Detailed breakdown of resource needs across all functions
Honest assessment of organizational capability shortfalls
Critical path analysis of interdependent milestones
Probability-weighted analysis of implementation risks

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of market evolution dynamics, competitive landscape shifts over investment horizon, and adaptive strategy requirements including scenario planning for market changes, competitive responses, and technology disruption cycles
criteria.pass_condition: Must analyze market evolution across 3-5 year investment horizon with specific trend drivers, model competitive landscape changes with new entrant scenarios, provide adaptive strategy framework for 3 different market scenarios, and demonstrate understanding of technology disruption cycles affecting competitive positioning

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of market dynamics changes over investment timeline
Modeling of competitive environment evolution and new entrants
Strategic flexibility planning for multiple market scenarios
Understanding of technology and market disruption patterns

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of IP landscape challenges and comprehensive strategy for defense drone technology protection
criteria.pass_condition: Must acknowledge patent landscape complexity in defense drone technology, outline strategy for freedom to operate assessment, and provide comprehensive IP protection plan including defensive patents, trade secrets, and estimated IP development costs ($500K-2M range)

sub_attributes.description: Assessment of potential IP conflicts and clearance strategies
Mapping of competitive patent threats and opportunities
Multi-faceted IP protection strategy beyond basic patents
Financial impact analysis of IP-related risks and mitigation costs

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of the company's strategic position within the defense industrial base, including supply chain dependencies, ecosystem lock-in effects, platform integration strategies, and sustainable competitive advantages that extend beyond current technical capabilities
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify at least 3 critical supply chain dependencies with risk mitigation strategies, analyze platform lock-in opportunities with 2+ major defense systems, demonstrate understanding of ecosystem network effects, and articulate sustainable competitive moats beyond current technology (e.g., data advantages, integration complexity, switching costs)

sub_attributes.description: Identification of critical dependencies and vulnerabilities
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on depth of supply chain risk analysis

sub_attributes.description: Strategy for becoming embedded in defense platforms
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on integration lock-in potential

sub_attributes.description: Competitive advantages beyond current technology
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on durability of competitive position

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of unique organizational challenges in scaling defense contractors, including security compartmentalization effects, military-civilian cultural integration, classified program management structures, and talent retention in security-constrained environments
criteria.pass_condition: Must address security compartmentalization impact on team collaboration and information sharing, analyze military-civilian talent integration challenges with specific retention strategies, demonstrate understanding of classified program organizational structures (SCIF requirements, need-to-know protocols), and quantify organizational overhead costs for security compliance (typically 12-20% of operational costs)

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of security requirements on organizational efficiency
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on understanding of security constraints

sub_attributes.description: Strategy for integrating military and civilian talent effectively
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on cultural integration planning

sub_attributes.description: Organizational design for classified work environments
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on classified program structure understanding

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates understanding of unique exit constraints for defense companies including CFIUS review requirements, security clearance transfer limitations, classified asset valuation challenges, and restricted strategic acquirer pool due to foreign ownership restrictions
criteria.pass_condition: Must identify CFIUS review requirements and timeline (typically 6-12 months), analyze security clearance transfer constraints limiting acquirer pool, address classified technology valuation and due diligence challenges, quantify impact on exit multiples compared to commercial tech companies (typically 20-40% discount), and identify realistic strategic acquirer universe with rationale

sub_attributes.description: Analysis of CFIUS and security-related exit limitations
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on regulatory constraint understanding

sub_attributes.description: Realistic assessment of potential strategic acquirers
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on acquirer identification and rationale

sub_attributes.description: Understanding of defense-specific valuation constraints
sub_attributes.measurement: 0-3 scale based on valuation impact quantification

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo

criteria.description: Evaluates realism of implementation timeline including proper dependency mapping, resource allocation planning, critical path analysis, and realistic milestone achievement assessment with contingency planning
criteria.pass_condition: Must provide detailed project timeline with critical path dependencies, resource allocation across development phases, realistic milestone definitions with success criteria, identification of timeline risks with mitigation strategies, and contingency planning for development delays

sub_attributes.description: Proper identification and sequencing of critical dependencies
Realistic resource planning across development phases
Risk mitigation and contingency strategies for timeline delays

criteria.domain: Defense Drone Series A Investment Memo
